title: mentha × dumetorum var. pubescens
intro: Mentha × dumetorum var. pubescens, commonly known as the hairy or pubescent wild mint, is a variant of the Mentha dumetorum plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid due to its high concentration of menthol and other volatile oils. The health benefits of Mentha × dumetorum var. pubescens include relief from nausea, indigestion, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate various health problems.
